chapter_question: What changes when straightness is intrinsic rather than Euclidean?
inspection_targets: Angle sum separates flat, spherical, and hyperbolic models.
Spherical excess becomes an area measurement.
Bending can change the embedding while preserving measured distances.
A developed strip tests whether the path was straight on the surface.

source_span.focus: Introduces intrinsic geometry by contrasting Euclidean, spherical, and hyperbolic behavior through geodesics and angular excess.
source_span.label: Chapter 01
source_span.title: Euclidean and Non-Euclidean Geometry
topics: Euclid's parallel axiom and its geometric consequences
Hyperbolic geometry as a coherent alternative to Euclidean geometry
Spherical great circles as straightest paths
Intrinsic versus extrinsic viewpoints on curved surfaces
Geodesics as shortest and locally straight paths
Sticky-tape and string constructions for physical intuition
